panels.title: CPU load by app — %core (busy = work it does; steal = host throttling). Watch step-ups against Deploys.
panels.description: Every app is shared-cpu-1x, so 100 = one full core. '<app> busy' is the work it does (user+system+io); '<app> steal' is CPU the host withheld because demand crossed the shared-CPU credit — near-zero historically, it only appears when busy saturates the core. A step-up that lines up with a purple Deploy marker means that release added load. (schowek excluded — it's a separate app.)

panels.title: CPU credit balance (serving apps — alarm < 500)
CPU throttled (rate, fraction of wall-clock)
CPU steal %
Memory available % (alarm < 8%)
HTTP latency — web (s)
Worker — throttled (1 = reapers backing off, 0 = full enqueue)
Instance up
Residential proxy — fetches served vs failed (recent 30 min, from web /metrics)
panels.description: The Decodo residential-proxy leg of the cinema scrape, surfaced from the in-app /uptime health (Mongo) via web's /metrics scrape. A flip from all-green 'served by proxy' to all-red 'failed → Zyte fallback' is a silent cost regression — scraping keeps working via Zyte, so no cinema bar goes red. Drives the 'Residential proxy failing' alert.

panels.title: Worker — task flow (per min): enqueued → started → fully worked
panels.description: The deferred-work pipeline on kinowo-worker, from worker /metrics. enqueued = new tasks added to the queue (dedup collapses excluded); started = tasks claimed by a worker; fully worked (done) = handlers that did real work (excludes 'skipped' freshness no-ops). In steady state the three track; started ≫ done means lots of skipped/rescheduled, done ≪ enqueued means a growing backlog.

panels.title: Worker — enqueued (non-duplicate) by type (per min)
panels.description: Rate of tasks ADDED to the queue per task type (kinowo_worker_tasks_enqueued_total{result="added"} — re-enqueues that the dedupKey collapses are excluded, so this is net new work, not churn). The per-type split of the 'enqueued' line in the overall flow panel above. Read with 'started by type' and 'fully-worked throughput by type' as one type's enqueue → claim → done funnel.

panels.title: Worker — started (claimed) by type (per min)
panels.description: Rate of tasks CLAIMED by a worker per task type (kinowo_worker_tasks_started_total). Counts every claim, including ones that then skip (data already fresh) or reschedule, so started ≫ the same type's 'fully-worked throughput' means that type is handing out a lot of no-op work. The per-type split of the 'started' line in the overall flow panel above.

panels.title: Worker — fully-worked throughput by type (per min)
panels.description: rate of done (not skipped) tasks per task type — which kinds of work the worker is actually completing: cinema scrapes, detail enrichment, TMDB/IMDb resolution, the four rating sources, and the staging-incubation steps.

panels.title: Worker — task outcomes (per min)
panels.description: Every handler return, by outcome: done (real work), skipped (data already fresh — the queue handed out work that was no longer needed), rescheduled (transient retry/backoff), failed (handler threw), no_handler (claimed mid-deploy on a node without the handler). A spike in skipped/rescheduled is wasted churn; failed is the error signal.

panels.title: Worker — fully-worked task duration (done, all types)
panels.description: Handler wall-clock of done tasks, percentiles over the bucketed histogram (kinowo_worker_task_duration_seconds). Skipped/failed runs are excluded. A rising p95/p99 means the upstreams (cinema sites, TMDB/IMDb, Filmweb/RT/Metacritic) or the scrape are getting slower.

panels.title: Worker — queue depth
panels.description: Live size of the durable task queue: waiting (claimable) + worked_on (leased by a worker right now), from worker /metrics. worked_on tracks pool utilization against pool size (the worker runs poolSize workers, one task each). A climbing 'waiting' is a backlog the pool isn't draining — the thing to correlate with the worker CPU-credit/steal panels above.

panels.title: Worker — waiting tasks by type
panels.description: Current backlog broken down by task type (sampled from the bounded active snapshot). Shows WHICH work is piling up — e.g. a wave of staging-incubation steps, or rating refreshes backing up behind a slow source.

panels.title: Worker — oldest waiting task age by type (s)
panels.description: Head-of-line latency: how long the oldest still-waiting task of each type has sat in the queue. A steadily climbing line is starvation — that type's work isn't being picked up (no handler, perpetual backoff, or the pool saturated by another type).

panels.title: Worker — fully-worked task duration p95 by type (s)
panels.description: Per-type tail latency of done (not skipped) tasks: the 95th-percentile handler wall-clock for each task type, over the bucketed histogram (kinowo_worker_task_duration_seconds). Splits the overall percentiles panel above by task_type so a slowdown localises to one upstream — e.g. ScrapeCinema (a cinema site), the *Rating types (Filmweb/RT/Metacritic/IMDb), or TMDB/IMDb resolution. Change 0.95 → 0.50 for the median.

panels.title: Worker — fully-worked task duration p50 (median) by type (s)
panels.description: Per-type typical latency of done (not skipped) tasks: the 50th-percentile (median) handler wall-clock for each task type, over the bucketed histogram (kinowo_worker_task_duration_seconds). The median companion to the p95-by-type panel above — p50 reads the everyday cost of each work kind, p95 the tail.

panels.title: Worker — staging incubation: movies by step (total = all in staging)
panels.description: Films currently incubating in pending_movies, stacked by the step each needs NEXT on its way into the live `movies` collection: detail (a cinema's synopsis/poster still owed) → resolve_tmdb (no TMDB match yet) → resolve_imdb (TMDB hit, IMDb cross-ref pending) → fold (concluded, about to graduate). Distinct films (a film's multiple cinema rows count once). The stacked total is the total movie count in staging; a step that keeps growing is where incubation is stalling (correlate with the staging task queue panels + StagingStuckAlerter).

panels.title: Worker — movie-row merges by reason & splits (per min)
panels.description: Rate of movie rows folded into another row per reason (kinowo_worker_merges_total — one increment per VICTIM absorbed; a cluster of N counts N−1). canonicalize=periodic same-film settle/rehydrate fold (cross-language same-tmdbId rows, or a ±year/spelling drift of one title); resolved-settle=a yearless stray absorbed when TMDB concludes a year; tmdb-identity=the runtime put-time same-tmdbId gate; normalize-rebuild=a title-rule change re-keys rows that now share a key and unions them. Each fold abandons the victim's title|year key, orphaning its DETAIL freshness — so a canonicalize spike here precedes the EnrichDetails → ResolveTmdb re-enrichment cascade that throttled the worker on 2026-06-19. The splits series (kinowo_worker_splits_total) is the inverse — rows a title-rule change un-merged, counted as new rows spawned (a 1→N split = N−1), each born fresh and re-resolving; normalize-rebuild merges are the re-merge half of a rebuild, splits the un-merge half.

panels.title: kinowo — films served per city (all future showings)
panels.description: Distinct films the web is currently serving on each /:city/ page (every film with a future showing), sampled once a minute from the real serving path (toSchedules) and exposed on web /metrics as kinowo_web_movies_served{scope="all"}. A city dropping to 0 here is the read-model-outage signal (a malformed web_movies row empties the whole corpus). The MoviesServedSwing + MoviesServedCityEmpty alerts watch this series.

panels.title: kinowo — films served per city (showing tomorrow)
panels.description: Distinct films each /:city/ page shows for tomorrow (≥1 showtime on the next local calendar day, Europe/Warsaw), from kinowo_web_movies_served{scope="tomorrow"}. Same per-minute sample as the all-future panel; the next-day view that fills the gap as today's showings pass.

panels.title: kinowo — films the movies collection would serve per city (all future)
panels.description: The SOURCE-collection mirror of the web panel above. Worker-side count of distinct films the live `movies` collection would serve on each /:city/ page (every film with a future showing), sampled off-band every 5 min by WorkerSourceFilmsMetrics through the REAL projection path and gated on the same readyToProject rule the projector writes by — exposed on worker /metrics as kinowo_worker_movies_served{scope="all"}. Compare against kinowo_web_movies_served{scope="all"}: in steady state the two match per city, so a city where worker > web means the read model (web_movies) has drifted behind the corpus — a stuck projection or a malformed/pruned web_movies row.

panels.title: kinowo — films the movies collection would serve per city (showing tomorrow)
panels.description: Source-collection mirror of the web tomorrow panel above, from kinowo_worker_movies_served{scope="tomorrow"} (worker-side, projected from `movies`, sampled every 5 min). Compare against kinowo_web_movies_served{scope="tomorrow"}; a per-city gap is the same read-model-drift signal as the all-future pair.

panels.title: Worker — movies corpus coverage (records, ids, ratings)
panels.description: Census of the live `movies` collection, sampled off-band every 5 min by WorkerCorpusMetrics and exposed as kinowo_worker_corpus_movies{subset}. total = distinct movie records; with_any_rating = at least one of imdb/fw/rt/mc; with_tmdb_id / with_imdb_id = resolution coverage; imdb_rating/rt_rating/mc_rating/fw_rating = per-source rating coverage. The gap between total and with_*_id is the resolution backlog; the gap between with_tmdb_id and each rating series is each site's enrichment backlog.
